lessons.title: The Specification Mindset
topics: Why beginners get stuck: diving into syntax without a plan ("the syntax woods")
What spec-driven development is and why it works for beginners and professionals alike
AI as architect vs. magic code button: the critical distinction
The "blueprint" model: how a specification functions as a step-by-step logical map
Activity: Reflection exercise — given two developer scenarios (one who codes immediately, one who writes a spec first), learners identify which produces better outcomes and explain why

lessons.title: Anatomy of a Specification
topics: The four components of a complete spec: inputs, outputs, logic, and edge cases
Use cases: describing what a user does, step by step, in a proposed system
Requirements language: precise, testable, and implementer-neutral
Reading a spec as a coding manual: how each requirement maps to an implementation step
Activity: Analysis exercise — learners review a sample specification and label each section; identify what is missing and propose additions
objects: SCORM: Interactive — Anatomy of a Specification
Assessment: Quiz: Anatomy of a Specification

lessons.title: Collaborating with AI to Write a Spec
topics: Prompting strategies: asking AI to decompose a problem, not solve it
Reviewing AI output: identifying what is precise, what is vague, and what is missing
Identifying and filling edge cases: "what happens when…?" as a discipline
Iterating toward a complete specification: the prompt–review–refine loop

Activity: Guided code-along — learners follow a structured prompting sequence against a provided problem statement to produce a first-draft specification, then critique it against the four-component checklist from Lesson 2
objects: SCORM: Interactive — Collaborating with AI to Write a Spec
Assessment: Quiz: Collaborating with AI to Write a Spec

lessons.title: From Specification to Implementation Plan
topics: Reading a spec as a coding roadmap: one requirement, one implementation step
Translating specification steps into pseudocode or ordered implementation notes
Staying the pilot: when and how to override or correct AI suggestions
Use cases as a verification tool: does the implementation plan satisfy every use case?
Activity: Independent exercise — learners receive a complete specification for a simple program and produce a numbered implementation plan without writing any code
objects: SCORM: Interactive — From Specification to Implementation Plan
Assessment: Quiz: From Specification to Implementation Plan
